segments.s674-g33: سپهبد is the subject (a title, 'spahbed'). فرود آمد is a compound verb meaning 'descended' or 'came down' (فرود as noun/verb 'descent' + آمد, past tense 'came'). از is the preposition 'from' and takes تخت شاد as its object: تخت 'throne' with the adjective شاد 'happy/rejoicing' following the noun (classical Persian word order: noun + adjective). Together this segment means the spahbed came down from the throne in a joyful state.
segments.s674-g34: همه شب is an adverbial phrase 'all night' / 'throughout the night.' ز (poetic form of از) introduces the person remembered (ز هرمز = 'of/from Hormoz' = 'about Hormoz'). یاد کردن is the construction 'to remember' where یاد (memory/remembrance) combines with کردن; here همی‌کرد is the older/imperfective past form (همی- prefix + کرد) meaning 'kept doing' or 'was remembering' in the third-person singular. So the clause says he kept remembering Hormoz all night.
segments.s674-g25: کرا is an archaic relative/pronominal 'who (ever)' placed before the noun phrase; گوهر تن is a genitive-like compound 'the jewel (گوهر) of the body/person (تن)' meaning a precious person; بود is the past copula 'was'; با + نژاد here gives an ablative/instrumental sense 'by/through (one's) lineage' (i.e. 'by birth'). The whole segment describes someone as being, by noble birth, a 'jewel among people.'
segments.s674-g26: نگوید functions as a classical third‑person negative present (poetic) meaning 'does not speak' / 'will not speak'; it takes سخن ('speech, a word') as its object. با کسی means 'with anyone' and جز introduces the exception 'except'; بداد here is presented as the exceptional addressee/name (in the poem it reads as a name or title). The clause therefore says that such a person utters no speech to anyone except that one.
segments.s674-g27: نباشد here is a negative jussive/optative form meaning 'may there not be / you shall not have'; شما را uses شما with the postposition را to mark the person affected ('for you'); جز از ایمنی literally 'except from security' = 'except safety' or 'but security.' This is a formulaic blessing/wish: 'may you have nothing but safety.'
segments.s674-g28: نیازد here is the third‑person (poetic) present meaning 'does not need / will not need' (or a jussive 'may you not need'); بکردار is a compound adjective 'of/with deed(s)' (meaning 'in deed' or 'by action'); آهرمنی is an adjective/attributive formed from Ahriman (the Devil) meaning 'Ahrimanic, demonic.' Together the clause says one will have no need of demonic deeds—i.e. one will not require the help/actions of Ahriman.
segments.s674-g29: هرآنکس که is a standard relative 'whoever that'; بشنید is the past tense 'heard' (third person), and گفتار شاه is the noun phrase 'the king's speech' (گفتار = 'speech, utterance', شاه = 'king'). This is a relative clause meaning 'whoever heard the king's words.'
segments.s674-g30: همی is a frequentive/continuous prefix here giving 'would/always' (classical/prosodic function); آفرین is the exclamation/acclamation 'bravo' (not the verb 'to create' here); خواند is the past tense 'uttered/called out'; برتاج is a prepositional compound 'upon the crown' (i.e. concerning his crown/sovereignty); و گاه links 'and (his) fortune' (گاه = 'time/fortune' in older usage = 'fortune'). The segment says listeners would continually shout praise over his crown and fate.
segments.s674-g31: برفتند is the past plural 'they departed/went away'; شاد is the predicative adjective 'joyful' modifying the subject; از بر تخت او is an older locative/ablative phrase 'from before his throne' (از بر = 'from in front of/of the'); the whole phrase means 'they left happily from his throne.'
segments.s674-g32: بسی is an adverbial intensifier 'much/very' (here 'many' or 'greatly'); آفرین is 'acclamation/praise' (noun); بود is the past copula 'was'; بر بخت او = 'upon his fortune' (بر marking the locus/target, بخت = 'fate/fortune'). Together: 'there was great acclaim upon his fortune.'
segments.s674-g1: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when' introducing a temporal clause. خسرو is the proper name (Khosrow). نشست is a simple past verb 'sat / took his seat.' برتخت زر is a compressed prepositional phrase 'on the golden throne' (برتخت = 'on the throne', زر = 'gold'), so the whole segment reads 'When Khosrow sat on the golden throne.'
segments.s674-g2: برفتند is past, 3rd-person-plural, 'they went / came (up/forward)' (بر- gives a sense of upward/forward motion). هرکس که is a relative construction 'whoever / each person who'. بودش is the old-style compound with enclitic -ش (بود + -ش) meaning roughly 'was to him' → 'had' (i.e. 'possessed'), and هنر here means 'merit / excellence'. Together: 'whoever had merit came forward.'
segments.s674-g3: گرانمایگان is a plural noun 'the illustrious / high‑born persons.' را is the Persian definite/object marker attaching to the object, and همه 'all' emphasizes 'all of them.' خواندند is past 3rd-plural 'they called / summoned.' The word order (object before verb) is normal in Persian.
segments.s674-g4: بر + آن = 'on that'; تاج نو = 'new crown' (تاج = crown, نو = new). گوهر افشاندند is a verb phrase 'they scattered / sprinkled jewels' (گوهر = gems, افشاندند = past 3rd-plural 'they scattered'). The particle بر marks the location/target of the scattering: 'they scattered jewels on that new crown.'
segments.s674-g5: به موبد 'to the mobad' (به = to/for, موبد = Zoroastrian priest). چنین گفت is 'said thus' — چنین = 'thus/so' and گفت here is the past verb 'he said' (the provided lexical senses list does not show the verbal sense, so see note). کاین تاج وتخت begins the reported clause: کاین = 'that/this', تاج = 'crown', وتخت = 'and the throne' (و + تخت).
segments.s674-g6: نیابد is the present/future subjunctive/inexact form of یابیدن used impersonally here meaning 'will not be found/attained.' مگر = 'except / unless,' مردم = 'people,' and نیک‌بخت = 'fortunate (people)' (nominative/indefinite). So the clause says 'will not be attained except by fortunate people.'
segments.s674-g7: مبادا is a jussive/optative particle 'may it not be' / 'lest.' The next item in the given word list (م) does not match the surface text here (morphologically the original has مرا 'me' or 'my'), so the given lemma senses do not fit (see senses). را is the object marker attached to 'me' (مرا). پیشه = 'profession / practice / craft.' جز = 'except / other than.' راستی = 'truth / uprightness.' The whole clause is a jussive wish: 'May my practice be nothing except uprightness.'
segments.s674-g8: که introduces the explanatory clause 'for / because that.' بیدادی = 'injustice / tyrannical wrongdoing.' آرد is a present/future‑oriented present stem meaning 'brings' (from آوردن). همه = 'all / every,' and کاستی = 'lack / deficiency.' So: 'for injustice brings every deficiency.'
segments.s674-g17: سوم = 'thirdly' (an ordinal/discourse marker). دور بودن is the infinitive 'to be distant / keep away' (verb after the adverbial). ز is the poetic form of از, 'from'. چیز کسان = 'the matters/things of people' — the ezafe is omitted in poetry, so چیز governs کسان (i.e. 'people's affairs').
segments.s674-g18: که introduces a relative clause modifying 'چیز کسان' ('which/whose'). دودش = دود (smoke) + enclitic -ش 'its/their'. بود is the past copula 'was'. سوی = 'toward(s)'; آنکس = 'that person'. رسان is a participial/agentive form 'causing to reach / reaching' used predicatively: 'whose smoke was reaching toward that person.'
segments.s674-g19: که again introduces a relative clause. در گاه و بی‌گه = 'at the proper time and at an untimely time' (paired adverbials). کسی را بسوخت is transitive: کسی + object marker را attached to the verb (رابسوخت) — بسوخت is simple past 'burned' (i.e. 'he burned someone' or 'someone heeded was burned').
segments.s674-g20: به marks cause/aim here: 'for/on account of'. بی‌مایه چیزی = 'a thing of no value' (adjective + noun). دلش = دل + enclitic -ش 'his heart'. برفروخت is a classical idiom (بر + فروخت) meaning 'his heart was set aflame / he became passionately attached' rather than literal 'sold' — past tense.
segments.s674-g21: دگر = 'other/otherwise' (discourse marker: 'as for the rest'). هرچ = 'whatever/anything'. در مردمی = 'among the people'; در خورد is a classical verbal phrase where خورد (from خوردن) means 'befalls/comes to pass' (not 'eat' here). Word order places the locative before the verb, typical in older Persian.
segments.s674-g22: مر = 'to/for me' (beneficiary pronoun). آن را = 'that' + object marker را. پذیرنده = 'one who receives / accepts' (participle/noun). باشد is the optative/subjunctive 'let it be / should be'. خرد = 'wisdom' (predicate). The clause says roughly 'that it have an acceptor for me is wisdom' → 'it is wise that someone accept it for me.'
segments.s674-g23: نباشد is a negative optative/subjunctive 'let there not be' (i.e. 'do not let there be'). مرا (appearing here as م + را in the tokenization) = 'me' (object). با کسی = 'with anyone / by anyone'. داوری = 'judgment' (noun). The whole phrase means 'let there be no judgment of me by/with anyone' (do not judge me by others).
segments.s674-g24: اگر = 'if' / 'for' (here causal/explanatory: 'for' / 'since'). تاج = 'the crown' (metonym for sovereignty/ambition). جوید‌گر is an agentive/participle 'one who seeks' (جوئیدگر/جویدگر). انگشتری = 'a ring' (small object); the line asserts a moral: 'a seeker of the crown will even desire a ring' — i.e. someone who craves great power will also covet small things, so comparisons/judgments are misleading.
segments.s674-g9: ابا هرکسی رای ما آشتیست functions as a single clause: رایِ ما ('our counsel/plan') is the predicate nominal, with آشتیست ('is reconciliation/peace') as the copular predicate. هرکسی ('anyone, everyone') is the scope of that counsel. ابا here appears before هرکسی and is not a modern preposition; none of the provided senses fit exactly (see senses mapping). Word order: topic/particle (ابا) + scope (هرکسی) + head noun phrase (رایِ ما) + predicate (آشتیست).
segments.s674-g10: ز + verbal noun پیکار کردن introduces a causal/ground notion ('from/for waging battle, to make war'); سرماتهیست is a predicate adjective (–ست copula fused) meaning 'empty‑headed/foolish' or 'resulting in ruin'. The clause states consequence/judgment about fighting: 'from fighting (it is) folly.'
segments.s674-g11: ز یزدان ('from God') marks the source with the poetic preposition ز. پذیرفتم is the first‑person singular past of پذیرفتن ('I accepted/received'). این تخت نو is the direct object ('this new throne'), with the demonstrative این before the noun and the adjective نو after the noun, a common classical word order.
segments.s674-g12: همین روشن ('this very bright/clear') begins a verbless descriptive tag that continues with مایه ('source/cause/resource') and وربخت نو, a compound notion relating to fate/fortune. و is the coordinating 'and'. The whole segment apposes/qualifies the throne as 'this very bright thing and the source of new fortune.' Note: وربخت in the edition here is lexicalized and treated as a unit (see senses).
segments.s674-g13: شما نیز ('you also') is the addressed subject plus the adverb 'also.' دلها is the plural direct object 'hearts.' بفرمان is a prepositional/compound expression meaning 'by order' or 'by command,' and دهید is the present/subjunctive/imperative 2nd‑person plural of دادن used here to mean 'cause/give' — together 'order or govern the hearts.'
segments.s674-g14: بهرکار is an adverbial/nominal phrase meaning 'for the work/for the service' or 'useful (work)'; بر ما is the prepositional phrase 'to/us'; سپاسی is the noun 'thanks/gratitude'; نهید is the 2nd‑person plural imperative/subjunctive of نهادن used as a verb 'place/give' — together: 'give us thanks for the work.' Word order places the object (سپاسی) before the verb نهید, as common in classical Persian.
segments.s674-g15: از + infinitive آزردن ('from hurting/offending') expresses prohibition/advice ('refrain from hurting'). مردم پارسا is the noun + adjective 'pious people' (پارسا qualifies مردم). The clause literally reads 'from hurting pious people.'
segments.s674-g16: و دیگر links the second item in the prohibition ('and also/other'); کشیدن سر ('to pull/draw the head') + از + پادشا is an idiomatic phrasing meaning 'to remove/behead the head of the king' (سر is the direct object, پادشا is the source/agent marked by از). The whole segment continues the admonition: 'and (from) again/also beheading a monarch.'

sentences.s674-s17: The spahbed descended from the throne, rejoicing; all night long he kept remembering Hormoz.
sentences.s674-s13: Whoever was a jewel of the body by birth, speaks no word with anyone except Bedad.
sentences.s674-s14: May you have nothing except security; you will not need the deeds of Ahriman.
sentences.s674-s15: Whoever heard the king's speech would always cry 'Bravo!' over his crown and fortune.
sentences.s674-s16: They departed joyful from before his throne; there was much acclaim on his fortune.
sentences.s674-s1: When Khosrow took his seat on the golden throne, whoever possessed merit came forward.
sentences.s674-s2: They summoned all the illustrious men and scattered jewels over that new crown.
sentences.s674-s3: He said to the mobad (priest) thus: 'This crown and throne will not be attained except by fortunate people.'
sentences.s674-s4: May my practice be nothing but uprightness; for injustice brings every deficiency.
sentences.s674-s9: Third: keep away from the affairs of people whose smoke reaches that person.
sentences.s674-s10: who, at seasonable and untimely times, burned someone; for a worthless thing his heart was kindled.
sentences.s674-s11: As for anything else that befalls among the people, it is wisdom that someone should accept it for me.
sentences.s674-s12: Let there be no judgment of me with anyone; for one who seeks the crown will even seek a ring.
sentences.s674-s5: Our policy is to make peace with everyone; waging war is foolish.
sentences.s674-s6: From God I received this new throne — this very bright seat and the source of a new fortune.
sentences.s674-s7: You too, command the hearts; grant us thanks for our service.
sentences.s674-s8: Refrain from hurting pious people, and likewise from beheading a monarch.
